# StorageProgram — Free Monad DSL Reference

A `StorageProgram<A>` is a pure data description of storage operations
that produces a value of type `A` when interpreted. Handlers build
programs using DSL functions; the interpreter executes them against
a real ConceptStorage backend.

## Core DSL Functions

| Function | Effect | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `createProgram()` | — | Create empty program |
| `get(p, relation, key, bindAs)` | read | Read one record |
| `find(p, relation, criteria, bindAs)` | read | Query by criteria |
| `put(p, relation, key, value)` | write | Create/update record |
| `merge(p, relation, key, fields)` | read+write | Partial update |
| `del(p, relation, key)` | write | Delete record |
| `branch(p, condition, then, else)` | both | Conditional logic |
| `pure(p, value)` | — | Terminate with value |
| `complete(p, variant, output)` | — | Terminate with tracked variant |
| `compose(first, bindAs, second)` | both | Monadic bind |

## Lens Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `relation(name)` | Lens focusing on a storage relation |
| `at(lens, key)` | Narrow to a specific record |
| `field(lens, name)` | Narrow to a specific field |
| `composeLens(outer, inner)` | Compose two lenses |
| `getLens(p, lens, bindAs)` | Read through a lens |
| `putLens(p, lens, value)` | Write through a lens |
| `modifyLens(p, lens, fn)` | Read-modify-write through a lens |

## Transport Effects

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `perform(p, protocol, op, payload, bindAs)` | Abstract transport effect |
| `performFrom(p, protocol, op, payloadFn, bindAs)` | Transport from bindings |

## Binding-Aware Variants

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `putFrom(p, relation, key, valueFn)` | Put with value from bindings |
| `mergeFrom(p, relation, key, fieldsFn)` | Merge with fields from bindings |
| `delFrom(p, relation, keyFn)` | Delete with key from bindings |
| `mapBindings(p, fn, bindAs)` | Pure computation on bindings |
| `pureFrom(p, fn)` | Terminate with value from bindings |

## Effect Set & Purity

```typescript
interface EffectSet {
  reads: ReadonlySet<string>;       // Relations read
  writes: ReadonlySet<string>;      // Relations written
  completionVariants: ReadonlySet<string>; // Variant tags
  performs: ReadonlySet<string>;     // protocol:operation pairs
}

type Purity = 'pure' | 'read-only' | 'read-write';
```

- `purityOf(program)` — derive purity from structural effects
- `classifyPurity(program)` — with fallback for deserialized programs
- `validatePurity(program, declared)` — check declared vs actual

## Example: Functional Create Handler

```typescript
import { createProgram, find, put, branch, complete } from '../runtime/storage-program';

const createUser = (input: Record<string, unknown>) => {
  const name = input.name as string;
  const email = input.email as string;
  const id = input.user as string;

  let p = createProgram();
  p = find(p, 'user', { email }, 'existing');

  const alreadyExists = createProgram();
  const done = complete(alreadyExists, 'error', { message: 'email taken' });

  const doCreate = createProgram();
  const created = put(doCreate, 'user', id, { name, email });
  const ok = complete(created, 'ok', { user: id });

  return branch(p, (b) => (b.existing as unknown[]).length > 0, done, ok);
};
```

## Example: Lens-Based Access

```typescript
import { createProgram, getLens, modifyLens, complete, relation, at, field } from '../runtime/storage-program';

const updateEmail = (input: Record<string, unknown>) => {
  const userId = input.user as string;
  const newEmail = input.email as string;
  const userLens = at(relation('user'), userId);
  const emailLens = field(userLens, 'email');

  let p = createProgram();
  p = getLens(p, userLens, 'current');
  p = modifyLens(p, emailLens, () => ({ email: newEmail }));
  return complete(p, 'ok', { user: userId });
};
```
